TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: told by Giɫgiɫaw of Vaɫulu village

S.FORM: ti Tjikunal kirimu a seman tjalupun tua sunat, sa tjalupunan nimadju.
S.PHON: ti cikunaɭ kirimu a səman caɭupun tua sunat, sa caɭupunan nimaɉu.
S.FORM: ti Tjikunal kirimu a seman tjalupun tua sunat, sa tjalupunan nimadju.
S.PHON: ti ʦikunaɭ kirimu a səman ʦaɭupun tua sunat, sa ʦaɭupunan nimaɉu.
S.TRANSL: Tjikunal straightaway made a hat out of paper and put it on.

S.FORM: ka manu sikiqaiɫungan, nasaregem aravats timadju.
S.PHON: ka manu sikiqaiʎuŋan, nasarəgəm aravaʦ timaɉu.
S.FORM: ka manu sikiqailjungan, nasaregem aravac timadju.
S.PHON: ka manu sikiqaiʎuŋan, nasarəgəm aravaʦ timaɉu.
S.TRANSL: When he looked in the mirror he looked very splendid.

S.FORM: qau "nanguaq a kiqetsi aken tua tsumay," aya timadju.
S.PHON: qau "naŋuaq a kiqəʦi akən tua ʦumaj," aja timaɉu.
S.FORM: qau "nanguaq a kiqeci aken tua cumay," aya timadju.
S.PHON: qau "naŋuaq a kiqəʦi akən tua ʦumaj," aja timaɉu.
S.TRANSL: So he said: “I must go and hunt a bear.”

S.FORM: seman tjakit tua zaman sa pasivuluq tua qau timadju a sema kasasavan.
S.PHON: səman cakit tua zaman sa pasivuɭuq tua qau timaɉu a səma kasasavan.
S.FORM: seman tjakit tua zaman sa pasivuluq tua qau timadju a sema kasasavan.
S.PHON: səman ʦakit tua zaman sa pasivuɭuq tua qau timaɉu a səma kasasavan.
S.TRANSL: He had miscanthus grass for a sword and held a bit of bamboo as a spear, and went out into the forecourt of the house.

S.FORM: manu patsun ti Kaɫikaɫi a kaka nimadju.
S.PHON: manu paʦun ti kaʎikaʎi a kaka nimaɉu.
S.FORM: manu pacun ti Kaljikalji a kaka nimadju.
S.PHON: manu paʦun ti kaʎikaʎi a kaka nimaɉu.
S.TRANSL: His sibling Kaɫikaɫi saw him,

S.FORM: "uri kemuda sun, kaka?" aya kivadaq.
S.PHON: "uri kəmuda sun, kaka" aja kivadaq.
S.FORM: "uri kemuda sun, kaka?" aya kivadaq.
S.PHON: "uri kəmuda sun, kaka" aja kivadaq.
S.TRANSL: and asked: “What are you going to do, brother?”

S.FORM: manu tevelain ni Tjikunal: "uri kiqetsi aken tua tsumay," aya.
S.PHON: manu təvəɭain ni cikunaɭ: "uri kiqəʦi akən tua ʦumaj," aja.
S.FORM: manu tevelain ni Tjikunal: "uri kiqeci aken tua cumay," aya.
S.PHON: manu təvəɭain ni ʦikunaɭ: "uri kiqəʦi akən tua ʦumaj," aja.
S.TRANSL: Tjikunal answered: “I’m going to hunt a bear.”

S.FORM: qau "su sikuda zua zaman azua qau?" ayain ni Kaɫikaɫi.
S.PHON: qau "su sikuda zua zaman azua qau" ajain ni kaʎikaʎi.
S.FORM: qau "su sikuda zua zaman azua qau?" ayain ni Kaljikalji.
S.PHON: qau "su sikuda zua zaman azua qau" ajain ni kaʎikaʎi.
S.TRANSL: So Kaɫikaɫi said: “What are you doing with that grass and bamboo?”

S.FORM: "inika zaman, inika qau.
S.PHON: "inika zaman, inika qau.
S.FORM: "inika zaman, inika qau.
S.PHON: "inika zaman, inika qau.
S.TRANSL: He answered: “That’s not grass or bamboo;

S.FORM: ku tjakit aitsu, ku vuluq aitsu," aya temvela.
S.PHON: ku cakit aiʦu, ku vuɭuq aiʦu," aja təmvəɭa.
S.FORM: ku tjakit aicu, ku vuluq aicu," aya temvela.
S.PHON: ku ʦakit aiʦu, ku vuɭuq aiʦu," aja təmvəɭa.
S.TRANSL: this is my sword and my spear.”

S.FORM: "iya, ui ḍi.
S.PHON: "ija, ui ɖi.
S.FORM: "iya, ui dri.
S.PHON: "ija, ui ɖi.
S.TRANSL: “Oh, I see.

S.FORM: pakianu aken, kaka," ayain ni Kaɫikaɫi.
S.PHON: pakianu akən, kaka," ajain ni kaʎikaʎi.
S.FORM: pakianu aken, kaka," ayain ni Kaljikalji.
S.PHON: pakianu akən, kaka," ajain ni kaʎikaʎi.
S.TRANSL: Take me with you, brother,” said Kaɫikaɫi.

S.FORM: manu kemim tiamadju tua tsumay.
S.PHON: manu kəmim tiamaɉu tua ʦumaj.
S.FORM: manu kemim tiamadju tua cumay.
S.PHON: manu kəmim tiamaɉu tua ʦumaj.
S.TRANSL: So they went looking for bears,

S.FORM: neka.
S.PHON: nəka.
S.FORM: neka.
S.PHON: nəka.
S.TRANSL: but didn’t find any.

S.FORM: qau "ainu anga, kaka, a tsumay?" aya ti Kaɫikaɫi.
S.PHON: qau "ainu aŋa, kaka, a ʦumaj" aja ti kaʎikaʎi.
S.FORM: qau "ainu anga, kaka, a cumay?" aya ti Kaljikalji.
S.PHON: qau "ainu aŋa, kaka, a ʦumaj" aja ti kaʎikaʎi.
S.TRANSL: So Kaɫikaɫi said: “Where are the bears, brother?”

S.FORM: "uzing anga, palamu anga," ayain ni Tjikunal.
S.PHON: "uziŋ aŋa, paɭamu aŋa," ajain ni cikunaɭ.
S.FORM: "uzing anga, palamu anga," ayain ni Tjikunal.
S.PHON: "uziŋ aŋa, paɭamu aŋa," ajain ni ʦikunaɭ.
S.TRANSL: “Over there; we’ll soon see them,” said Tjikunal.

S.FORM: manu ka tsuay a pagaɫu tevuta zua niamadju a ngiaw.
S.PHON: manu ka ʦuaj a pagaʎu təvuta zua niamaɉu a ŋiaw.
S.FORM: manu ka cuay a pagalju tevuta zua niamadju a ngiaw.
S.PHON: manu ka ʦuaj a pagaʎu təvuta zua niamaɉu a ŋiaw.
S.TRANSL: After quite a time their cat emerged.

S.FORM: "aku tja ngiaw, kaka.
S.PHON: "aku ca ŋiaw, kaka.
S.FORM: "aku tja ngiaw, kaka.
S.PHON: "aku ʦa ŋiaw, kaka.
S.TRANSL: “Why, it’s our cat, brother.

S.FORM: maya qemtsiqetsi," aya ti Kaɫikaɫi.
S.PHON: maja qəmʦiqəʦi," aja ti kaʎikaʎi.
S.FORM: maya qemciqeci," aya ti Kaljikalji.
S.PHON: maja qəmʦiqəʦi," aja ti kaʎikaʎi.
S.TRANSL: Don’t kill it,” said Kaɫikaɫi.

S.FORM: ɫakua sipatsiɫik sakamaya ni Tjikunal sa laingi.
S.PHON: ʎakua sipaʦiʎik sakamaja ni cikunaɭ sa ɭaiŋi.
S.FORM: ljakua sipaciljik sakamaya ni Tjikunal sa laingi.
S.PHON: ʎakua sipaʦiʎik sakamaja ni ʦikunaɭ sa ɭaiŋi.
S.TRANSL: But Tjikunal just raised his weapons and chased it.

S.FORM: ti Kaɫikaɫi "maya maya, a tja ngiaw, kaka, semalimsim," aya a patjaviliviliɫ.
S.PHON: ti kaʎikaʎi "maja maja, a ca ŋiaw, kaka, səmaɭimsim," aja a pacaviɭiviɭiʎ.
S.FORM: ti Kaljikalji "maya maya, a tja ngiaw, kaka, semalimsim," aya a patjavilivililj.
S.PHON: ti kaʎikaʎi "maja maja, a ʦa ŋiaw, kaka, səmaɭimsim," aja a paʦaviɭiviɭiʎ.
S.TRANSL: Kaɫikaɫi followed him saying: “Don’t don’t; it’s our cat, brother; the poor thing.”

S.FORM: ka ika makalaing ti Tjikunal, semutjalupun anga sa veritsan anga a zaman a qau a djinadjas, sa tjumaq anga.
S.PHON: ka ika makaɭaiŋ ti cikunaɭ, səmucaɭupun aŋa sa vəriʦan aŋa a zaman a qau a ɉinaɉas, sa cumaq aŋa.
S.FORM: ka ika makalaing ti Tjikunal, semutjalupun anga sa verican anga a zaman a qau a djinadjas, sa tjumaq anga.
S.PHON: ka ika makaɭaiŋ ti ʦikunaɭ, səmuʦaɭupun aŋa sa vəriʦan aŋa a zaman a qau a ɉinaɉas, sa ʦumaq aŋa.
S.TRANSL: When Tjikunal failed to catch up with the cat, he took off his hat and threw away the grass and bamboo he had been holding, and went indoors.

S.FORM: manu pakatjaraitan nua kina tua vurasi a kinuɫaw tiamadju a marekaka.
S.PHON: manu pakacaraitan nua kina tua vurasi a kinuʎaw tiamaɉu a marəkaka.
S.FORM: manu pakatjaraitan nua kina tua vurasi a kinuljaw tiamadju a marekaka.
S.PHON: manu pakaʦaraitan nua kina tua vurasi a kinuʎaw tiamaɉu a marəkaka.
S.TRANSL: So their mother gave the siblings a roasted sweet potato each.

S.FORM: ka kemakan tiamadju.
S.PHON: ka kəmakan tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: ka kemakan tiamadju.
S.PHON: ka kəmakan tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: While they were eating,

S.FORM: mangtjez azua ngiaw niamadju; ɫakua seqaɫanga sakamaya, ika ui anga a ɫiadet.
S.PHON: maŋcəz azua ŋiaw niamaɉu; ʎakua səqaʎaŋa sakamaja, ika ui aŋa a ʎiadət.
S.FORM: mangtjez azua ngiaw niamadju; ljakua seqaljanga sakamaya, ika ui anga a ljiadet.
S.PHON: maŋʦəz azua ŋiaw niamaɉu; ʎakua səqaʎaŋa sakamaja, ika ui aŋa a ʎiadət.
S.TRANSL: their cat came in; but it was very shy and wouldn’t go near them.

S.FORM: "qau pai pasedjaluu, kaka, a tja ngiaw," ayain anga ni Kaɫikaɫi ti Tjikunal.
S.PHON: "qau pai pasəɉaɭuu, kaka, a ca ŋiaw," ajain aŋa ni kaʎikaʎi ti cikunaɭ.
S.FORM: "qau pai pasedjaluu, kaka, a tja ngiaw," ayain anga ni Kaljikalji ti Tjikunal.
S.PHON: "qau pai pasəɉaɭuu, kaka, a ʦa ŋiaw," ajain aŋa ni kaʎikaʎi ti ʦikunaɭ.
S.TRANSL: Kaɫikaɫi said to Tjikunal: “Make friends with our cat, brother.”

S.FORM: sa pasedjalu-i anga, sana ɫiadedet, sana kakan tua sinitseviq ni Tjikunal.
S.PHON: sa pasəɉaɭu-i aŋa, sana ʎiadədət, sana kakan tua siniʦəviq ni cikunaɭ.
S.FORM: sa pasedjalu-i anga, sana ljiadedet, sana kakan tua siniceviq ni Tjikunal.
S.PHON: sa pasəɉaɭu-i aŋa, sana ʎiadədət, sana kakan tua siniʦəviq ni ʦikunaɭ.
S.TRANSL: So he made friends with it, and then it came near and ate the bits of food which Tjikunal broke off for it.
